title: Number of Islands
prompt:
Given a 2D grid of '1's (land) and '0's (water), return the number of islands.

An island is formed by connecting adjacent lands horizontally or vertically. Diagonal connections do not count.
You may mutate the grid while solving.
If the grid is empty, return 0.
constraintQuestions: Can we mutate the grid? (Assume yes.)
What should we return for an empty grid? (0.)
Are values strings or numbers? (Often strings, but either is fine if handled.)
Should we aim for O(r*c) time?

walkthrough.title: Sequential scan + BFS/DFS flood fill
walkthrough.body:
Scan every cell.
When you see land, increment the island count and flood-fill to mark all connected land as water.
This prevents double counting.

codeSnippets.title: JavaScript
C#

walkthrough.title: Complexity
walkthrough.body:
Time: O(r*c) because each cell is visited once.
Space: O(max(r, c)) to O(r*c) depending on queue usage.
